reasoning_summary: The ViewStatement activity is exported and receives an untrusted URL from Intent extras ('uname') without validation, then passes it directly to WebView.loadUrl(). No sanitizers (e.g., URL whitelist, scheme checks) were detected in the taint path. This matches the documented behavior of InsecureBankV2 and satisfies the exported-component + untrusted-input-to-WebView pattern.
evidence_for: Taint flows from Intent.getStringExtra("uname") directly to WebView.loadUrl() without intermediate validation
No sanitizer (URL whitelist, scheme block, host check) matched along the propagation path
APK is a known vulnerable sample (InsecureBankV2) used for security training and tool validation
evidence_against: If the WebView had JavaScript disabled and only loaded HTTPS URLs, impact would be lower; however, no such hardening was observed

reasoning_summary: MyBroadCastReceiver is exported with an intent-filter and reads 'phonenumber' from an untrusted Intent, forwarding it to SmsManager.sendTextMessage(). The app holds SEND_SMS permission. No permission checks, caller validation, or input sanitizers were found in the taint path. This is a textbook example of an exported component abusing a dangerous permission.
evidence_for: Receiver <com.android.insecurebankv2.MyBroadCastReceiver> is exported=true and has intent-filter action 'theBroadcast'
Taint path shows Intent.getStringExtra("phonenumber") -> SmsManager.sendTextMessage() without validation
No sanitizer matched; no explicit permission check (e.g., checkCallingPermission) in the propagation path
evidence_against: On modern Android versions (API 26+), implicit broadcasts to manifest-registered receivers are restricted, reducing attack surface; however, the app targets API 22 where this restriction does not apply

reasoning_summary: Same exported broadcast receiver reads 'newpass' from an untrusted Intent and forwards it to SmsManager.sendTextMessage(). This is a second source-to-sink path within the same vulnerable component. No sanitizers or permission guards were detected.
evidence_for: Same exported receiver with intent-filter action 'theBroadcast'
Taint path shows Intent.getStringExtra("newpass") -> SmsManager.sendTextMessage() without validation
No sanitizer or permission check in the propagation path
evidence_against: Modern Android implicit broadcast restrictions reduce exploitability on API 26+, but the app targets API 22
